prompt:
User request:
Generate a scatter plot of sales vs customer_age colored by region with a Streamlit selectbox for product

Available columns:
product: category
revenue: float
region: category
sales: float
customer_age: int
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.title("Sales vs Customer Age")

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["product"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_product = st.selectbox("Product", options)
if selected_product == "All":
    df_widget = df
else:
    df_widget = df[df["product"] == selected_product]

chart = px.scatter(
    df_widget,
    x="customer_age",
    y="sales",
    color="region",
    template="plotly_white"
)

st.plotly_chart(chart, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.header("Units by Segment")

chart = px.box(

)

prompt:
User request:
Plot a bar chart of min sales by month where region == South

Available columns:
date: datetime
month: category
region: category
year: int
sales: float
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Min Sales by Month")

df_filtered = df[df["region"] == "South"]

df_plot = df_filtered.groupby("month", as_index=False, observed=True)["sales"].min()

chart = px.bar(
    df_plot,
    x="month",
    y="sales",
    title="Min Sales by Month (South Region)"
)

st.plotly_chart(chart, use_container_width=True)

prompt:
User request:
Build a histogram of revenue

Columns in df:
units: int
year: int
revenue: float
customer_age: int
segment: category
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Distribution of Revenue")

fig = px.histogram(
    df,
    x="revenue"
)

fig.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a line chart of mean customer_age over date

Available columns:
date: datetime
customer_age: int
product: category
segment: category
year: int
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Mean Customer Age over Date")

df_plot = df.groupby("date", as_index=False)["customer_age"].mean()

fig = px.line(
    df_plot,
    x="date",
    y="customer_age",
    title="Mean Customer Age over Date"
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Show a line chart of mean units over month colored by segment where segment == Home Office with a Streamlit selectbox for segment

units: int
month: category
segment: category
sales: float
date: datetime

User request:
Plot a bar chart of mean sales by region

Available columns:
month: category
sales: float
date: datetime
revenue: float
region: category
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.subheader("Mean Sales by Region")

df_plot = df.groupby("region", as_index=False)["sales"].mean()

fig = px.bar(
    df_plot,
    x="region",
    y="sales",
    labels={"sales": "Mean Sales", "region": "Region"}
)

fig.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

prompt:
User request:
Plot a histogram of customer_age colored by product with a Streamlit selectbox for product

Available columns:
customer_age: int
units: int
product: category
revenue: float
profit: float

User request:
Generate a bar chart of min sales by segment with a Streamlit selectbox for product

Columns in df:
segment: category
product: category
sales: float
year: int
region: category
output:
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px

st.header("Min Sales by Segment")

options = ["All"] + sorted(df["product"].dropna().unique().tolist())
selected_product = st.selectbox("Product", options)
if selected_product == "All":
    df_widget = df.copy()
else:
    df_widget = df[df["product"] == selected_product].copy()

df_plot = df_widget.groupby("segment", as_index=False)["sales"].min()

chart = px.bar(
    df_plot,
    x="segment",
    y="sales",
    title="Min Sales by Segment"
)

chart.update_layout(height=500)

st.plotly_chart(chart, use_container_width=True)
